title: general updates
body: flipped breifly through entries in an old journal. It is entirely possible I may be an insane person. But also starting to get the notion that I do some of my best writing when in these strange mental states. best to keep it under wraps for now.

as i jotted down earlier, out of principle I do not go back and read my jounral entries. First, why would I read the ramblings of an insane person? and second, even if there are glimpses of clarity in there, wouldn't reading and reflecting on my own work lead to a usless cycle of increasingly derivative and insipid walls of text? metacognition is scary and therefore retarded. theres something odd about cringing every time I accidently peek at an old entry. thats like a cat getting spooked by its own reflection in a mirror. but i think in general its something that will improve as i read more and hopefully write more. Learning articulation and eloquence are the main goals here, being able to map 1:1 thoughts and arguments in my head that seem very well formed and the incoherent mess of words I speak or write. Until then, there are too many new thoughts to think and ramblings to ramble - I do not have the time nor inclination to sit back and actually grow as a person.
General Updates: (edit : captain updates finally made promotion) (edit 2: shut up)
-did not fail 5.601 (i mean, trivially a 59 is an "F" but this F stands for Fantastic! I don't fail the class")
-bailed on 7.19 presentation. dick move and I did feel bad but it wouldve legitimately killed me to give a shit, so really I had no choice

-Salinger is goated, finished Raise High the Roof Beams + Seymore: An introduction. As per fuckin usual had the "what if I was a short story writer" thought and then banished it to the shadow realm. [we're learning! yay :) ]
-Zootopia 2 was awesome
-pretty fun late night convo with C Will
-UQ Oschner application submitted! had mild panic attack yesterday about future, nothing new to worry about just same old same old.
-lubing up the ol imagination machine. big things to come.
god DAMN i need to fix the arrows on this thing Im sick of rewriting the same 3 shitass lines

-Sean if you sniped the url and are reading this, sneak the word "banana" into our next conversation. I ask you don't read it at all, but human nature is what it is. so as a foreword, the thoughts on these pages simultaneously are and are not reflective on me as a person. the point of this i think is trying to let out some the thoughts in my head so I don't literally explode.

-ran into Monty last night, had a great time catching up with him. I have a "pick up right where you left off" friendship with him, which is the very best kind in my opinion. he is a pretty cool dude. might be coming to 7.35 on monday with me
excuse the grammatical and spelling war crimes made here, i typed fast and poorly. I have a thermo Pset to copy due in 90 min and this entry has been made under duress.

title: its happening again
body: I gotta get rid of the forward and backward arrows on this thing, I keep accidentally clicking it and fucking up the whole post. or make an auto save every 30 seconds or something.

back in 7.19 with no idea what tf is going on. head has been swimming again lately, but I think that's probably some mix of the NyQuil + half sick/probably drinking mold from the water bottles I have not been cleaning properly (reminder to buy Dawn soap), overall poor diet and sleep in the past week, stressed tf out on Sunday for histories, plus haven't felt a ray of sunlight in weeks. cope, I guess.  The schizophrenic adventure and being born with a brain designed to eat berries in cave, forced to endure the psychotic hell of modern society. I hate this awful fuzzy dizzy brain feeling that happens sometimes. so hard to focus, its like im caught in a raging river, carried by trubulent flow between eddies and whirlpools, a maelstrom in my head that I can only hope to wait out. so lame

I keep coming back to the idea that one should pursue the skills and interests that they find they have a leg up on the average person on. Would help the decision process of the Big Question by pruning the things that Im already way behind on. like how I am interested in musical comedy (bo burn ham, ERB, flight of concords, ruck rucka, pink guy, etc) but I can't play an instrument or have never written a song in my life. sure I know how to be funny, but just too far behind the buck on that one. it should be a hobby but should not be prioritized to be a main quest rn. came I think with animation/cartoonist - mainly that I can't draw for shit if its not from reference. there's years and years of doodling and trial and error that go into finding ones own style artistically. So again, hobby and interest, yes, but not main goal. It's this constant problem I have of first: finding something I really enjoy, so much so that I can lose myself in it, if even for a moment. An immersion into the work that affords a momentary transcendence from the self, which in my opinion is the mark of true art.  Then, at some point, I get the thought that: hey, maybe I could do this too! which promptly ends my enjoyment of the aforementioned work since I can no longer interact with as a member of the audience, now, in my head, I am some potential artist of the same medium (which is not a thing, you are still an audience member, dumbass. have the prescience of mind to understand that half the assholes in a comedy club audience all think "yeah I could do standup comedy if I wanted, too" . it doesn't fucking matter. get that through your thick skill. something something about the man in the arena and all that). anyway, lets get out of my own ass for a sec. the girl in front of me has open a presentation for a group I am part of, that i did not think was due to be presented until Thursday. although, stepping back for a moment, its kinda funny that the idea of people doing work two (2) days before the deadline is so surprising to me that it did not cross my mind before the incorrect assumption that the due date is actually  today. lol. I said to myself that when I found a pursuit I truly care about, my 'passion' or 'purpose', I would stop procrastinating and work way before the deadline, going all in. so it might be a bad sign that by week 4 Im doing histories the morning of .

fundamentally there's a problem with the notion that I need to be adequately cultured before truly starting out on any creative/comedic/entertainment venture. which is ridiculous and a misled notion - you'll learn so much more along the way of actually doing the thing itself, (even if it ends up as shit, what matters is that you wholeheartedly try your best) than you would from methodically sampling a terrific magnitude of media that represents the acme of an entire generations body of work. as one of my favorite quotes on this topic says (from fucking reddit of all places): "Of course you'll be a midwit if all you ever do is consume, you can look at all the esoteric media in the world and still never reach the level of a 14 year old drawing anime fanart, because god is only found in the act of creation".

But its still an unshakeable thought. especially in these modern times that require a prepostourus degree of self-driven action to educate oneself on the arts, a new fact of life in the era of slop and short form dogs hit drivel. in the past, the time during which these books and movies and shows were all created, a product of their times, this culturization would have happened naturally, automatically to anyone who had their eyes and ears open and was simply alive and gave a shit. it was just part of the process. it may not be true but there is the prevailing notion (in the context of the MFA nonsense going around) that any author worth studying, whose writing will make you a better writer yourself, is dead. whether that's a bias from time exposed to the literary community - simply being around for longer allowing the cream of the crop to rise, or a valid indictment of modern literature, is up for debate. but consider the fact that nowadays there are not nearly the same number of famous poets, philosophers, scientists, or physicists known to the average member of the public not involved in their field. whose fault is that?

quick aside:
lol the guy presenting rn is floundering because his whole thing was done by AI and he hasn't done any preparation of his own. tough look when he can't explain the very words projectedon the screen above him that he supposedly wrote himself in measured prose. he awkwardly laughs as his embarrassment is laid bare - we all know it and but no one will say it. not a single thought has gone into this assignment. (ha, couldn't be me!)

Back to the good stuff. last night I was reading about Conan and his path through comedy, writing, and entertainment. I longed for a chance to go back and choose a different path, imagining late nights in the writers room of the Harvard Lampoon, riffing with my friends on ridiculous satires of The Crimson and the student leadership's most recent gaffs. [See: A Stupid and Futile Gesture (2018)]. It feels right. I don't wallow, because I know there is no way I possibly could have known. We are all products of our upbringing. There is no possible other path to the place I am now than the exact path I have taken. In the words of the thermodynamics class I have been skipping for the past month, my existence in the present is a path function, not a state function. Like heat or work, a fundamental property of the universe. So I can't change the past, all I can do is improve my present by pursuing the ideal future. It is exhausting going against the grain, leading a double life. I am strong enough to go all in on on my dreams, I know who I am at my core - but I don't entirely know what those dreams are. It's just a feeling deep in my gut. So I continue on, doing the bare minimum in the life I am supposed to want, the life that is supposed to be enough. on its own. millions of other people would dream of my current life. what gives me the fucking right, the audacity, to want something else entirely? I should be grateful. I took someone else's spot. (whatever). and on the other end, slowly trudging through a mountain of thinking and art that speaks to me at a human level. that I get stared at like an alien freak for wanting more than the meritocracy of stem career and the promise of cold hard cash. having made that decision and accepted that fundamental Truth, I do feel much lighter and free to live the life I actually want. that said, I can only imagine that A change of environment and being surrounded by a facilitative group of peers so that together we can rise in the ranks, would be a happier way to spend my time. ugh. I leave you with an excerpt from V. that spoke to me deeply.

“She knew instinctively: he will be fine as the fraternity boy just out of an Ivy League school who knows he will never stop being a fraternity boy as long as he lives. But who still feels he is missing something, and so hangs at the edges of the Whole Sick Crew. If he is going into management, he writes. If he is an engenieer or architect why he paints or sculpts. He will straddle the line, aware up to the point of knowing he is getting the worst of both worlds, but never stopping to wonder why there should ever have been a line, or even if there is a line at all. He will learn how to be a twinned man and will go on at the game, straddling until he splits up the crotch and in half from the prolonged tensión, an then he will be destroyed.”
― Thomas Pynchon, V.
sorry for rambling. at the very least, I do feel better for having written this. maybe I just needed to eat some breakfast.

title: uhOh
body: that was NOT good. I'd say I'm good for a solid 35 on that test with roughly another 15 on the line. I may have to rethink my rather cavalier attitude that I've had so far towards thermodynamics. Can't say I really thought through the whole If I Fuck this up I'm Actually Fucked part of this whole process. Its not unsalvageable unless the grade cutoffs and class average team up to dp me.

I'm struggling to think of a singularly worse way to spend the upcoming year than a ninth semester here . Perhaps being assraped by a clown that keeps asking me 'what my plans are for after graduation'. Poetically, though, there would be closure - the academic collapse would be complete. From the precocious gifted child with the world at his fingerprints to lazy chud who got held back in 16th grade. Noah Bryce's legacy must live on. Redownloading Fizz now to appeal to the masses to calm my worries.

title: Captain's Log, final hours
body: Morale is low. The end is upon us. As a final resort the crew has traded the last of our hardtack and water for a case of limited edition Ninja X Fortnite redbulls. May God save our souls.
cocky want Boing Boing

title: 7.19: Does Tylenol cause Autism? who gives a shit
body: goofin off on da computer.  thog do not care about GWAS.

Making eye contact and nodding my head periodically so that I blend in with the others and hopefully don't get called on. It's the awesome game I pay 80K a year for - I pretend to be interested and he pretends to not notice. whatever social contract me sitting in this room was ever meant to fulfill died years ago with the shortsighted introduction of technology into the classroom. but we play anyway. its all we know how to do. I obey the letter of the law, if not the spirit. it's ok, he seems content enough to listen to himself talk for two hours straight. Only God almighty knows what is going through his head during the awkward silences that grace the room every quarter hour or so. I will say however it was funny to see him dance around the racial implications of Spearman's G factor. I promise you A century ago this man would've absolutely been going across the country giving phrenology lectures on how the negroid contingency's feeble prefrontal cortexes simply can't handle complex tasks like rotating shapes or voting.

Fixed the checklist so its not totally broken. Not sure how I feel about comic sans font but we'll run with it for now. Strong serif for this page though because journaling is a serious matter. ho hum. maybe I am a journalist. I sure as shit ain't a biologist.

24 hrs until Thermo Midterm 1. Panic has not yet set in but the drums in the deep are beginning to rumble. The goblins are coming. all is not lost yet. remember: Under P/NR all things are possible. See you tomorrow when the storm has passed.

body: hello every1 welcome to da Big show
